scenario: Xander Healthcare runs Camunda 8.8 with 5 Spring Boot services that exchange 3500 tokens per hour and migrates from an 8.5 configuration that still defines common.keycloak.realm. The platform team must understand what the 8.8 reference says about replacing that legacy Keycloak-specific key.
question: How does the 8.8 reference describe the replacement for the legacy common.keycloak.realm key?

options.text: The 8.8 reference lists no replacement for common.keycloak.realm and notes that the OIDC issuer is provided as a URL in the unified configuration.
The 8.8 reference lists camunda.client.auth.realm as the replacement for common.keycloak.realm under the unified camunda.client configuration prefix.
The 8.8 reference lists camunda.client.auth.token-url as the replacement for common.keycloak.realm under the unified camunda.client configuration prefix.
The 8.8 reference lists common.keycloak.url as the replacement for common.keycloak.realm under the unified camunda.client configuration prefix.

a.text: Correct. The common.keycloak row for realm leaves the replacement column empty and adds the hint that there is no Keycloak-specific configuration for Camunda; the issuer is provided as a URL.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no camunda.client.auth.realm entry in the 8.8 reference; the unified configuration uses the issuer URL rather than a Keycloak realm name.

c.text: Incorrect. camunda.client.auth.token-url is the replacement for common.keycloak.token-url and common.keycloak.url, not for the realm row.

d.text: Incorrect. common.keycloak.url is itself a legacy key with its own replacement; it does not replace common.keycloak.realm in the 8.8 reference.

explanation: The common.keycloak.realm row in the 8.8 reference has an empty replacement column. The hint clarifies that there is no Keycloak-specific configuration for Camunda and that the issuer is provided as a URL.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
